title: phyllanthus simplex subvar. leiospermus
intro: Phyllanthus simplex subvar. leiospermus, also known as the "Small-fruit Phyllanthus" or "Amalaki," is a plant species native to Southeast Asia and is known for its small, green, and spherical fruits. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, anti-viral, and anti-bacterial properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids. The health benefits of Phyllanthus simplex subvar. leiospermus include its ability to protect against liver and kidney damage, reduce fever and inflammation, and support immune system function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often consumed as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts have been used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for various health conditions.
